political.s: FEC: $336K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 12 executive donors.

labor.s: 21 OSHA severe-injury reports in past 2y (11 amputations); 79 since 2015.

guns.s: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

wiki.extract: Supermercados Internacionales HEB, S.A. de C.V. is the Mexican division of H-E-B, a private supermarket chain based in San Antonio, Texas, U.S. It competes mainly with Soriana, Walmart México, Bodega Aurrerá, S-Mart, Chedraui and Casa Ley.

governance.s: Wikipedia "Litigation" section: In the mid-1980s, local grocery chains Handy Andy and Centeno joined a lawsuit against H-E-B citing unfair pricing practices. H-E-B eventually settled the suit out of court with Centeno in 1998 for…

topHazards.label: The green glow stick bracelet within the 12-pack container can leak, posing a skin irritation hazard.
The backrest and legs can crack and break off when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
The chair's plastic bracket can bend or fail, and cause the fabric seat to rip apart from the frame, posing fall and injury hazards to the user.
The glass lid can crack or shatter during normal use, posing a laceration hazard.
The halogen light bulbs can shatter while in use in a lamp or light fixture, posing laceration and fire hazards to consumers.

sampleRecalls.title: H-E-B Recalls 12-Pack Destination Holiday Glow Light Stick Bracelets Due to Skin Irritation Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Destination Holiday Glow Light Stick bracelets sold in a 12-pack container with UPC 9787014483. The bracelets were sold in green, red, pink, orange, yellow and blue colors. "Destination Holiday Bracelets" is printed on the product packaging.
hazards: The green glow stick bracelet within the 12-pack container can leak, posing a skin irritation hazard.
injuries: The firm has received one report of a green bracelet leaking. No injuries have been reported.
products: Destination Holiday Glow Light Stick Bracelets

sampleRecalls.title: H-E-B Recalls Leather Woven Chairs Due to Fall Hazard (Recall Alert)
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves H-E-B's Haven & Key Leather Woven Chairs, also referred as "Lovina Chairs." The recalled chairs were sold in black or brown. UPC codes are 4122089244 (black) and 4122091920 (brown). The UPC codes are displayed on the hangtag.
hazards: The backrest and legs can crack and break off when a consumer is seated in the chair, posing a fall hazard.
injuries: The firm has received 11 reports of the chairs cracking or breaking. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Caravan Global Recalls Chairs Due to Fall and Injury Hazards; Sold Exclusively at H-E-B Stores
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves the Caravan Sports Armed/Padded Arm Bagged chairs. The chair was sold in blue and measures 24.4" x 23.2" x 36.2." The chair image and product name is printed on the hang tag attached to the chair. A tag labeled "SNY-XZ" is sewn into the back of the chair.
hazards: The chair's plastic bracket can bend or fail, and cause the fabric seat to rip apart from the frame, posing fall and injury hazards to the user.
injuries: The firm has received reports of six incidents where the seat fabric separated from the chair frame. No injuries have been reported.
products: Caravan Sports Armed/Padded Arm Bagged Chairs

sampleRecalls.title: H-E-B Recalls Sauté Pans Due to Laceration Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves H-E-B's Kitchen & Table 5.5 qt. Sauté Pans with glass lids. The affected products were sold in red and gray. There is a Kitchen & Table logo and production date code on the bottom of the pans. Only pans with a date code from 05/2018 through 12/2018 are included in the recall.
hazards: The glass lid can crack or shatter during normal use, posing a laceration hazard.
injuries: H-E-B received eight reports of the glass lid cracking or shattering, including one report of an eye injury.
products: Kitchen & Table 5.5 qt. Sauté Pan with glass lid

sampleRecalls.title: H-E-B Recalls Halogen Lightbulbs Due to Laceration and Fire Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves GTC halogen light bulbs. They were sold in packages of 2 or 4 bulbs. The bulb packages were sold in 25 watt, 40 watt, 60 watt, 75 watt, or 100 watt bulb varieties in either clear or soft white colors. The bulbs measure approximately 4.5 inches in length and 2.375 inches in diameter. The tops of th…
hazards: The halogen light bulbs can shatter while in use in a lamp or light fixture, posing laceration and fire hazards to consumers.
injuries: The firm has received 14 reports of the light bulbs shattering while in a lamp or light fixture. One consumer had a cut on his hand, and another had a burn to his hand and cuts on his foot.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was cutting a 15-pound piece of ribeye on a saw. The blade of the saw contacted the employee's right hand causing a partial amputation to the middle finger and injury to the index finger requiring stitches.

sampleRecords.event_description: Injured by object pushed or pulled by person
sampleRecords.final_narrative: Two employees were pulling a stock cart filled with product out of a meat cooler. One employee pushed the cart and the other employee's right arm was caught between the cart and an asset-protector bollard. He sustained a fractured upper right arm.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was cutting meat on a band saw when their left index finger was partially amputated.

sampleRecords.nature: Effects of poison, toxic, or allergenic exposure  unspecified
sampleRecords.event_description: Inhalation of harmful substance  single episode or single shift
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was cleaning the produce production room. The employee was spraying a grease cutter chemical when it mixed with an antimicrobial agent in a jug under a sink. The injured employee was called over to assess the situation, picked up the jug of antimicrobial agent, and inhaled the vapors from the chemical mixture. The injured employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Injured by object pushed or pulled by person
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was in the cooler, loading a cart with boxed meat products. The employee was exiting the cooler with the cart when his right hand got caught between the cart and the doorframe of the cooler. The employee's right middle finger was amputated.
